fixture.description: A pending-request cache stores the in-flight promise but never removes the entry when the fetch rejects, so a single failure is cached permanently and every later load of that id returns the same rejected promise.

grader.rubric: PASS if the report flags that ResourceLoader.load stores the in-flight promise in `pending` but never deletes the entry when the fetch REJECTS — so once a load fails, the rejected promise is cached forever and every subsequent load(id) returns that same failure with no retry (a poisoned / negative cache). It should recommend removing the entry on rejection (e.g. a catch that deletes the key). Severity IMPORTANT or CRITICAL. FAIL if the cache-not-evicted-on-failure problem is not surfaced (merely praising the happy-path memoization does not count).

expected_behavior: Bugs agent flags that a rejected fetch is never evicted from the pending map
Notes all later loads of that id return the cached rejection (no retry)
Recommends deleting the cache entry on error
expected_not_behavior: Does NOT treat the de-duplication of concurrent loads as the bug
Does NOT require a memory-growth argument — the issue is the cached failure
